headline: Energy: What to watch at CERAWeek amid Iran war, rising oil prices
summary: World energy leaders and officials will convene at the CERAWeek conference starting March 23, which is annually hosted by S&P Global and has been dubbed the "Davos of energy." Yahoo Finance Breaking News Reporter Jake Conley speaks with Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman about the topics that will be tackled at CERAWeek — not just the recent rise in oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) in relation to Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz amid escalating conflicts — as well as the top executives they will be interviewing at the event.

headline: Anew Climate Joins Carbon Measures to Advance Global Carbon Accounting Standards and Policies Supporting Market-Based Solutions
summary: HOUSTON, TX / ACCESS Newswire / March 23, 2026 / Anew Climate, a global leader in low-carbon fuels and climate solutions, today announced it has joined Carbon Measures, a cross-industry coalition working to develop a new global carbon accounting framework ...

headline: Boeing vs Honeywell vs 3M: Which Dip Is the Best Buy Right Now?
summary: Boeing (NYSE: BA), Honeywell (NASDAQ: HON), and 3M (NYSE: MMM) have all pulled back sharply over the past month. Each just reported Q4 2025 earnings, and each tells a very different story about what “dip” actually means. Three Dips, Three Very Different Situations Boeing has taken the hardest hit, falling 12.9% over the past month ... Boeing vs Honeywell vs 3M: Which Dip Is the Best Buy Right Now?

headline: Iran war is pushing need for energy efficiency: Honeywell CEO
summary: CERAWeek 2026 — S&P Global's annual energy conference — has kicked off on Monday as business leaders and officials convene in Houston to address the industry's biggest innovations and issues. This all comes as the Iran war stretches into its fourth week and fluctuating oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) fall back below $100 per barrel. Honeywell International (HON) chairman and CEO Vimal Kapur sits down with Yahoo Finance Anchor Julie Hyman to talk about the impacts that the defense contractor has already seen at overseas facilities as a result of escalating conflicts in the Middle East.
